name: implementation-harness
description: Scaffold a Ralph-style single-loop, CI-gated TASKS.json implementation harness into a project's self-contained `.harness/` folder, author its backlog, and operate it — via skills. Worktree or in-place isolation (for projects whose build needs untracked local state), data-driven difficulty auto-tuning that starts every task at the cheapest tier and escalates up a global ladder, a portable zero-dependency dashboard (backlog, a rendered ideas inbox, per-facet harness internals — chosen model, policy + observed audit rate, failure health — and a live loop-status strip with freshness badges), an ideas-to-tasks pipeline, operational skills for pre-run vetting, interrupt recovery, and reviewing failed tasks into follow-ups, and an upgrade skill that reconciles an existing install with newer plugin versions — including adopting legacy or hand-forked installs. A `custom/` overlay makes project customization upgrade-safe — project conventions, lifecycle hooks, a secret-guard denylist, and visual-verify / build-audit prompt injection — surfaced through a guided customization walkthrough skill on create, on upgrade (only what's new since your version), or on demand. Distinct from the official ralph-loop plugin.
